scheme_name: Netaji Subhash Chandra Bose Boys Residential Hostel Scheme (Classes 1 to 8)
page_title: Netaji Subhash Chandra Bose Boys Residential Hostel Scheme (Classes 1 to 8)

details: The Netaji Subhash Chandra Bose Residential School Scheme for Boys (Class 1-8) is a centrally sponsored scheme launched in 2017-18. It targets vulnerable children like orphans, child laborers, street dwellers, and those with single parents, offering them free residential facilities to complete their primary education and reduce the male-female gap in education. The scheme operates in 52 districts with 100-bed hostels. Benefits include free stay, food, medical, books, uniforms, and daily necessities. Application is by approaching the District Project Coordinator/Warden.
benefits: These hostels admit orphans, homeless children, single parents, destitute child laborers, rag pickers, children living on railway station platforms and bus stands and provide them with residential facilities to complete their primary education.
Provide educational opportunities to children from educationally disadvantaged groups through residential schools.
Bridging the educational gap between boys and girls.
Provide residential school facilities to provide children with primary education.
eligibility: Boys who have dropped out of school because a middle school is not available within a 3 km radius or a high school/higher secondary school is not available within a 5 km radius of their village.
Boys identified as school dropouts and out-of-school shall be admitted to the Netaji Subhash Chandra Bose Residential Hostel.
Children from Scheduled Caste (SC) and Scheduled Tribe (ST) Ashram Schools that are at the primary level may also be admitted to the hostel.
Local resident boys shall not be admitted to the hostel.

offline: Application Method: To apply, one needs to ensure the child is provided with residential facilities to complete their primary education (कक्षा 1 से 8 तक).
Contact/Registration: Contact the District Project Coordinator/Warden
Application Fee: There is No application fee
Appeal: In case of an appeal, contact the District Project Coordinator's Office/District Education Centre.
Time Limit: The process is operational for the Full Year

documents_required: It should be provided as per the guidelines of the District Project Coordinator's Office/District.

faqs.question: What is the main objective of the scheme?
faqs.answer: The main objective is to provide residential education facilities and opportunities for primary education to vulnerable and deprived boys (Class 1-8) and to reduce the gap between male and female access to education.

faqs.question: Which classes are covered under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme covers students from Class 1 to Class 8.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to take admission in these hostels?
faqs.answer: Orphans, destitute, child laborers, single-parent children, and street/railway platform/bus stand dwelling children are eligible.

faqs.question: What facilities are provided to the children?
faqs.answer: The children receive free residence, food, clothing, books, uniforms, medical care, and daily-use items.

faqs.question: Do local resident children get admission?
faqs.answer: No, local resident children are generally not admitted to these hostels.

faqs.question: Is there any fee to apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, there is no application fee (

faqs.question: Whom should I contact for admission or registration?
faqs.answer: You should contact the District Project Coordinator/Warden.

faqs.question: Is the scheme operational only for a limited period in the year?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme is operational for the full year (

faqs.question: Is the benefit of the scheme only for boys?
faqs.answer: The scheme mentioned is specifically for Boys and aims to reduce the education gap.

faqs.question: What is the process to appeal a decision?
faqs.answer: An appeal can be made to the District Project Coordinator's Office/District Education Centre.
